final_review:
The advent of quantum computing has significant implications for cryptography, as it can potentially break certain classical encryption algorithms (Lenstra et al., 2001). In recent years, researchers have been exploring the applications of quantum computing in cryptography, particularly in the context of post-quantum cryptography (Fernández‐Caramés et al., 2020). The development of quantum-resistant cryptography is an active area of research, driven by the need to protect against potential quantum computing attacks. Quantum key distribution schemes offer unconditional security, making them an attractive option for secure communication (Gisin et al., 2002).

The rise of blockchain technology has also led to increased interest in the application of quantum computing to blockchain cryptography (Fernández‐Caramés et al., 2020). Blockchain technology has been widely adopted in various fields, including healthcare (Habib et al., 2022; Zhang et al., 2023), and its security is a major concern. The use of quantum computing can potentially break certain blockchain encryption algorithms, making it essential to develop quantum-resistant blockchain cryptography (Fernández‐Caramés et al., 2020). Researchers have been exploring the use of physical unclonable functions (Herder et al., 2014) and other techniques to enhance the security of blockchain technology.

The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) has been actively involved in the development of post-quantum cryptography standards (Alagic et al., 2022). The NIST post-quantum cryptography standardization process has been ongoing since 2016, and it has made significant progress in recent years (Alagic et al., 2022). The development of quantum computing in the NISQ era and beyond has also been an active area of research (Preskill, 2018).

In the context of 6G technology, researchers have been exploring the potential applications of quantum computing and blockchain technology (Jiang et al., 2021; de Alwis et al., 2021). The integration of these technologies is still in its early stages, and further research is needed to fully explore its potential. The use of quantum computing and blockchain technology can potentially enhance the security and efficiency of 6G networks.

Despite the progress made in recent years, there are still significant research gaps in the area of quantum computing and cryptography. The development of practical quantum-resistant cryptography and the integration of quantum computing and blockchain technology are open challenges. Further research is needed to address these challenges and to fully explore the potential of quantum computing in cryptography.

In conclusion, the application of quantum computing to cryptography has significant implications for the development of secure communication systems. The use of quantum-resistant cryptography and blockchain technology can potentially enhance the security of communication systems. However, there are still significant research gaps that need to be addressed.

References:
Alagic, G., Apon, D., & Cooper, D. A. (2022). Status report on the third round of the NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography Standardization process. https://doi.org/10.6028/nist.ir.8413
de Alwis, C., Kalla, A., & Pham, Q. V. (2021). Survey on 6G Frontiers: Trends, Applications, Requirements, Technologies and Future Research. https://doi.org/10.1109/ojcoms.2021.3071496
Fernández‐Caramés, T. M., & Fraga‐Lamas, P. (2020). Towards Post-Quantum Blockchain: A Review on Blockchain Cryptography Resistant to Quantum Computing Attacks. https://doi.org/10.1109/access.2020.2968985
Gisin, N., Ribordy, G., & Tittel, W. (2002). Quantum cryptography. https://doi.org/10.1103/revmodphys.74.145
Habib, G., Sharma, S., & Ibrahim, S. (2022). Blockchain Technology: Benefits, Challenges, Applications, and Integration of Blockchain Technology with Cloud Computing. https://doi.org/10.3390/fi14110341
Herder, C., Yu, M. D., & Koushanfar, F. (2014). Physical Unclonable Functions and Applications: A Tutorial. https://doi.org/10.1109/jproc.2014.2320516
Jiang, W., Han, B., & Habibi, M. A. (2021). The Road Towards 6G: A Comprehensive Survey. https://doi.org/10.1109/ojcoms.2021.3057679
Lenstra, A. K., & Verheul, E. R. (2001). Selecting Cryptographic Key Sizes. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00145-001-0009-4
Preskill, J. (2018). Quantum Computing in the NISQ era and beyond. https://doi.org/10.22331/q-2018-08-06-79
Zhang, W., Qamar, F., & Abdali, T. N. (2023). Blockchain Technology: Security Issues, Healthcare Applications, Challenges and Future Trends. https://doi.org/10.3390/electronics12030546
